name: Cocoa Chili
recipeInstructions:
People will probably be surprised to hear that the "secret" to this chili's great taste is cocoa. It adds a subtle rich flavor to slightly spicy chili.
In an 8-quart saucepan or kettle, combine water, undrained tomatoes and tomato juice; begin cooking over medium heat. Meanwhile, in a skillet, melt shortening. Add beef and onion. Cook, stirring occasionally, until browned; immediately add beef and onion to the tomato mixture. Add garlic, bay leaves, chili powder, cocoa, oregano, cumin, sugar, salt (up to 2), and cayenne. Heat to boiling. Reduce heat; simmer for 2-1/2 to 3 hours. Add additional water if the chili gets too thick during cooking. About 30 minutes before the chili is done, add cilantro. Mix flour with 1/3 cup cold water; stir in the chili. Continue to cook, stirring constantly until the chili thickens. Remove bay leaves before serving. Yield: 3-4 quarts.
